sections.heading: Systemic
sections.content: Systemic therapy is used in various settings: neoadjuvant for very-high-risk or laCSCC, adjuvant for extremely high-risk features, concurrent with RT for select laCSCC or regional disease, and alone for unresectable, recurrent, or metastatic disease. Immune checkpoint inhibitors (cemiplimab-rwlc, pembrolizumab) are preferred in many settings.

cards.name: Cisplatin
cards.detail: Setting: Systemic therapy for use with RT or surgery (Table 2). Key toxicities: Nephrotoxicity, neurotoxicity, ototoxicity, myelosuppression, nausea/vomiting.

cards.name: Carboplatin
cards.detail: Setting: Systemic therapy for use with RT or surgery (Table 2). Key toxicities: Myelosuppression (particularly thrombocytopenia), nephrotoxicity.

cards.name: Carboplatin/Paclitaxel
cards.detail: Setting: Systemic therapy for use with RT or surgery (Table 2) or alone (Table 3). Key toxicities: Myelosuppression, neuropathy, alopecia, hypersensitivity reactions.

cards.name: Cetuximab
cards.detail: Setting: EGFR inhibitor for use with RT or alone (Table 2 and 3). Key toxicities: Infusion reactions, acneiform rash, hypomagnesemia, diarrhea.

cards.name: Cisplatin/Fluorouracil
cards.detail: Setting: Useful in certain circumstances (Table 2 and 3). Key toxicities: Not specified in provided text.

timeline.title: 1st Line
timeline.body: Cemiplimab-rwlc or Pembrolizumab (if curative RT or surgery is not feasible for locally advanced, recurrent, or metastatic disease).

timeline.title: 2nd Line
timeline.body: If ineligible for or progressed on immune checkpoint inhibitors: Carboplatin/Paclitaxel, Carboplatin/Paclitaxel + Cetuximab, EGFR inhibitors (e.g., Cetuximab). Useful in certain circumstances: Neoadjuvant Cemiplimab-rwlc, Capecitabine, Cisplatin, Cisplatin/Fluorouracil.
